definition: The current FCI-implied effective monetary policy stance is substantially looser than the nominal FFR of 4.25-4.50%, though the precise gap is unquantifiable. The direction is almost certainly correct but specific estimates (e.g., 150-250bp gap) are pseudo-precise because channels are nonlinear, interact, and operate on different time horizons.
evidence: All evidence points to effective policy being looser than nominal FFR
Goldman FCI easing despite 525bp of hikes
Specific 150-250bp quantification refuted in pair_0 debate as dimensionally confused
Cannot multiply policy rate by scalar transmission efficiency due to channel nonlinearity
open_questions: Can alternative measurement approaches quantify the gap without channel-linearity assumptions?
Does the gap narrow if AI equity valuations normalize?
